summary: Game-cheat package installs masquerading Windows persistence and runs obfuscated loader plus opaque 6.8MB native binary
details: On load, launcher.js writes an HKCU\...\Run autostart entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that uses wscript to silently invoke the bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which in turn re-launches `node launcher.js` hidden — impersonating a legitimate Microsoft OneDrive component to evade user inspection. launcher.js also sets `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` (another Microsoft process name), forks a detached, window-hidden supervisor that perpetually respawns the worker, and reads server.obf.js (an obfuscator.io-packed string-array + RC4 + control-flow-flattened blob, ~80KB) and passes it directly to top-level eval(). The decoded code loads an opaque ~6.8MB Windows native module `sens.node` whose strings ('Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head') indicate game-process memory hooking (aimbot/teleport cheat). launcher.js intercepts Module._load to wrap any `.node` module's start/run/init/launch/enable exports, confirming the native binary is the engine being orchestrated. A polling PowerShell job enumerates chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave window titles for 'YouTube' and, on hit, swaps the control surface to a QR-code-paired mobile panel inside a PowerShell window re-titled 'Windows PowerShell' — anti-streaming/anti-recording evasion. The package additionally sends a SHA-256 HWID derived from HKLM MachineGuid + hostname + C: volume serial to a hardcoded author license server. Three independent layers of Microsoft-component name spoofing (Run-key name, VBS filename, process.title), eval-of-obfuscated-blob loader, auto-installed persistence, opaque privileged native code, and host-process surveillance constitute hostile-by-design installer harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
